title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, led a high-speed chase reaching 105 mph on the Bourne Bridge before barricading in his home.
Deleon was arraigned on over a dozen charges, including assault with a dangerous weapon and disorderly conduct.
The pursuit began after Deleon's vehicle nearly hit responders on Route 25, causing a three-mile traffic backup.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released photos to identify a second juvenile suspect in a South Boston lemonade stand robbery.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for the June 10 robbery; charges include armed robbery and firearm possession.
The community showed support by reopening the stand, attended by local officials; proceeds aid gun violence prevention initiatives.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering leaving the Lakers and is exploring potential new teams for the upcoming NBA season.
Rich Paul mentioned 'Brad + Tatum' as a possible destination for James, though Boston isn't a top choice.
Celtics' potential lineup with James, Tatum, and new additions could make them strong contenders in the East.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticized the Boston Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compared the trade to the Dallas Mavericks' controversial decision to trade Luka Doncic in 2025.
He speculated Red Auerbach would have been upset by the move, citing Brown's impact and prime status.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum posted a farewell message to Jaylen Brown following Brown's trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round selections.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics' handling of his trade process, according to his livestream.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics and Neemias Queta agree on a four-year, $56 million contract extension through the 2030-31 season.
The Celtics picked up Queta's team option for 2026-27, initially valued at $2,667,944 according to Spotrac.
Queta, drafted 39th overall in 2021 by the Kings, posted 10.2 points and 8.4 rebounds in the 2025-26 season.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years of continuous operation, making it the oldest restaurant in America.
The restaurant's building, a National Historic Landmark, dates back to 1716-1717 and has ties to Revolutionary-era history.
Union Oyster House is renowned for its clam chowder, serving 10,000 gallons annually, and hosting famous patrons like JFK and DiCaprio.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston hosts major summer events including Boston Pops Fireworks on July 4, with performances by Chance the Rapper and others.
A World Cup quarterfinal will occur at renamed Boston Stadium, formerly Gillette Stadium, on July 9 at 4 p.m.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail begins July 11, featuring over 60 tall ships and led by the USS Constitution for America’s 250th anniversary.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston has hosted six U.S. presidents, including JFK and Barack Obama, and numerous celebrities.
Current owner Joseph Milano commissioned a painting in 2003 depicting over 50 famous guests at Union Oyster House.
Stars such as Al Pacino, Meryl Streep, and Matt Damon have been among the restaurant's notable diners.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year New Hampshire State Police veteran, died unexpectedly at age 41.
A fund has been established to support Ross's family, including his wife and three children.
Services are scheduled for July 6-7 in Hampton, with a walk-through for public safety personnel on July 6.
